---
title: "Open VSCode"
software:
- OpenVSCode 
- conda
- poetry
- uv
login:
- webapp-sso
- cli
os_flavor: linux
os:
- Ubuntu 22.04
data:
- ResearchDrive
- irods-icommands
packages: []
gpu: false
admin: true
support: UU
---

## Description

A fully functional VSCode integrated development environment (IDE) in the browser, based on the fully open-source version of [VSCode](https://code.visualstudio.com/).

Login is done via [SRAM](https://servicedesk.surf.nl/wiki/spaces/IAM/pages/74226073/SURF+Research+Access+Management).
The workspace also installs additional development tools:

 -  [`conda`](https://www.anaconda.com/docs/getting-started/miniconda/main)
 -  [`poetry`](https://python-poetry.org/docs/)
 -  [`uv`](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/concepts/python-versions/)

Upon request we can help you to automate the installation of packages or automate other installations (e.g. tools for data transfer). Automating these steps will make it easier to work following our [tips for responsible use](../../responsible-use.qmd).

## Creation

{{< include ../partials/_storage.qmd >}}

{{< include ../partials/_creation.qmd >}}

{{< include ../partials/_request-access.qmd >}}

## Access
{{< include ../partials/_access-sso.qmd >}}

{{< include ../partials/_access-ssh.qmd >}}


{{< include ../partials/_data_transfer.qmd >}}

## Usage

### Working with Python Environments
This workspace includes `uv`, `conda`, and `poetry` for Python environment management. See the [Python environment manual](../../manuals/python-environment.qmd) for best practices and getting started.

### Terminal and Commands
Access the integrated terminal with `` Ctrl+` `` or via the Terminal menu. With sudo rights, you can install additional system packages using `sudo apt install`.

## Tips
{{< include ../partials/_sudo-rights.qmd >}}
